---
title: "Check Nodes Pipeline (T, R, Python, Bash)"
format: html
filters:
  - tlang
---

# Introduction

This report showcases a polyglot pipeline managed by **T**.
The pipeline involves nodes written in T, R, Python, and Bash, with data flowing between them using **Arrow** and **JSON**.

# Pipeline Overview

```{t}
#| label: pipeline-summary
#| echo: false
inspect_pipeline()
```

# Native T Data

The pipeline starts with a simple T list:

```{t}
#| label: data-t
read_node("data_t")
```

# R and Python Collaboration (Arrow)

An R node creates a dataset, and a Python node transforms it. Both use **Arrow** for efficient interchange.

## R Data (df_r)
```{t}
#| label: df-r
df_r_node = read_node("df_r")
# read_arrow reads the arrow artifact into a T dataframe
df_r = read_arrow(df_r_node.path)
head(df_r)
```

## Python Transformed Data (df_py)
```{t}
#| label: df-py
df_py_node = read_node("df_py")
df_py = read_arrow(df_py_node.path)
head(df_py)
```

# Models and Vectors

# R ggplot graph (plot_r)

This node produces a ggplot object, which is not natively supported for T deserialization.

```{t}
#| label: plot-r
read_node("plot_r")
```

## R Models

### Linear Model (lm_r)
This is an RDS-serialized R `lm` object.
```{t}
#| label: lm-r
read_node("lm_r")
```

### Generalized Linear Model (glm_r)
This is an RDS-serialized R `glm` object.
```{t}
#| label: glm-r
read_node("glm_r")
```

### PMML Linear Model (r_model_pmml)
This is a PMML-serialized R `lm` object.
```{t}
#| label: r-model-pmml
read_node("r_model_pmml")
```

### T-Native Prediction (pred_t_r)
This is a T-native prediction of the R PMML model.
```{t}
#| label: pred-t-r
read_node("pred_t_r")
```

## Python Models

### Linear Regression (lm_py)
This is a Pickle-serialized Scikit-learn model.
```{t}
#| label: lm-py
read_node("lm_py")
```

### Logit Model (logit_py)
This is a Pickle-serialized Statsmodels Logit model.
```{t}
#| label: logit-py
read_node("logit_py")
```

### PMML Linear Model (py_model_pmml)
This is a PMML-serialized Python model.
```{t}
#| label: py-model-pmml
read_node("py_model_pmml")
```

### T-Native Prediction (pred_t_py)
This is a T-native prediction of the Python PMML model.
```{t}
#| label: pred-t-py
read_node("pred_t_py")
```

## Simplified Summaries and Vectors

### R Model Summary (JSON)
```{t}
#| label: model-r-json
read_node("model_r_json")
```

### Python Vector (JSON)
```{t}
#| label: vector-py
read_node("vector_py")
```

# Bash node Summary

Finally, a Bash node that processed these paths:

```{t}
#| label: res-bash
read_node("res_bash")
```

# Conclusion

T makes it easy to orchestrate complex, multi-language workflows without worrying about the underlying serialization details for common formats.
